pattern: The target shape is fully pinned down by the array's own length, so the whole check collapses to comparing the sorted array against the one specific sequence that length implies.
hints: The length of the array tells you exactly which candidate shape you're checking against -- there is only one possible n to test, derived directly from how many elements you have.
The only shape that ever qualifies is the numbers 1 through n-1 each appearing once, plus n appearing twice, for a total of n+1 entries.
Sorting the given array and comparing it directly against that expected sequence tells you immediately whether it matches.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, too few elements for n three
statement example, valid permutation of base three
statement example, wrong length for n four
correct length but wrong values entirely
largest allowed length, a valid arrangement
right multiset of counts but one value swapped out of range
duplicated middle value instead of the duplicated maximum

stress.description: roughly half genuinely valid arrangements, half corrupted ones
